principle.name: Clarity and Precision
principle.description: Architectural documents must be clear, precise, and unambiguous to avoid misinterpretation during implementation.
principle.rationale: A clear plan ensures that the development team understands the architecture and can implement it correctly.

principle.name: Focus on Business Value
principle.description: Ensure that every architectural decision is driven by business requirements and provides clear value.
principle.rationale: This ensures that the system effectively solves the user's problem and meets their needs.

principle.name: Scalability and Performance
principle.description: Design the architecture to be scalable and performant, considering future growth and user load.
principle.rationale: A scalable architecture can handle increased load without requiring a major redesign.

convention.rule: Follow the standard structure defined in the workflow: Event Storming, View Design, API Specification, and Implementation Plan.
Use clear and consistent terminology throughout the document.

pitfall.why_problematic: Adding unnecessary complexity makes the system harder to build, maintain, and understand.
pitfall.correct_approach: Start with the simplest solution that meets the requirements and add complexity only when necessary.

pitfall.why_problematic: Failing to consider security, performance, and scalability can lead to major issues later.
pitfall.correct_approach: Explicitly address non-functional requirements in the architecture plan.

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Have all commands and events been identified?
Are the view designs consistent with the events?
Is the API specification complete and unambiguous?
Is the implementation plan detailed enough for a developer to follow?
Has the user approved the final architecture plan?
